data.title: Japan's Wage Growth Likely to Slow Due to Tariff Threat — Market Talk
data.description: Japan's pay growth will likely slow in the coming months as higher tariffs hit production and shipments, says Moody's Analytics economist Stefan Angrick. Inflation-adjusted wages dropped 1.8% from a year earlier in April, according to government data released Thursday. Continued inflation "spells trouble for household consumption and raises the stakes ahead of July's Upper House election," Angrick says in a research note. "With wage growth stumbling and inflation proving sticky, things will only get more difficult from here," he adds. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

"With wage growth stumbling and inflation proving sticky, things will only get more difficult from here," he adds. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Force Motors rise after May sales jump
data.description:
** Force Motors shares up 1% to 12,455; earlier rose as much as 4.1% to record high of 12,835 rupees

** Auto maker's total sales in May rise 19.1% Y/Y to 3,088 units; domestic sales surge 24.5%, while exports fall 52.2%

** FORC up nearly 94% YTD

data.title: Philippines' Headline Consumer Inflation Further Slows to 1.3% in May
data.description:
The Philippines' headline inflation eased to 1.3% in May from 1.3% in the previous month, according to data from the Philippine Statistics Authority on Thursday.

The lower inflation was mainly driven by a slower annual increment in the index of housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels at 2.3% in May from 2.9% in the previous month. Another contributor was a faster annual drop in the restaurant and accommodation services at 2% from 2.3% in the month prior.

Food inflation remained unchanged 0.7% in May versus April, the PSA said.

Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, remained unchanged at 2.2% in May versus the month prior, the data showed.

data.title: Worldline to Repurchase EUR320 Million of Convertible Bonds
data.description:
Worldline SA agreed to repurchase 320 million euros worth of its convertible bonds due July 30, 2026, after completing a reverse bookbuilding process on Wednesday, according to a same-day release.

The French payments company will buy back 3,221,238 bonds at 99.40 euros each, representing around 41.6% of the originally issued bonds with a principal amount of 332.4 million euros.

Settlement is expected on June 11, with the repurchased bonds to be cancelled.

data.title: Tech shares drive Hong Kong and China stocks higher
data.description:
Hong Kong and China stocks rose on Thursday, led by tech and artificial intelligence shares, as analysts said Hong Kong-listed tech firms remain under-represented in global AI investment portfolios.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 Index and the Shanghai Composite Index both edged up 0.1% by the lunch break. Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng was up 0.4%.

** Hong Kong's equity market offers strategic value for global investors, serving as a key option for diversifying portfolios and navigating the ongoing shift away from the U.S. dollar, Huatai analysts said in a note.

** The analysts said technology remains a central investment theme. "As future gains in global productivity are expected to hinge on advances in artificial intelligence, the companies best positioned to lead this race are largely concentrated in the U.S. and Hong Kong," they said, adding that Hong Kong remains under-allocated.

** Tech shares traded in Hong Kong were up 0.9%, tracking gains in Chinese ADRs listed in New York overnight, while onshore tech shares (.CSIASITI) climbed 1.0%.

** China's services activity expanded at a slightly faster pace in May, with new orders growing more quickly than in April, though new export orders declined due to uncertainty stemming from U.S. tariffs, a private sector survey showed on Thursday.

** The CSI Rare Earth Index rose 0.7%, after a group representing auto suppliers in the United States called for immediate action to address China's restricted exports of rare earths, minerals and magnets, warning the issue could quickly disrupt auto parts production.

data.title: China's Akeso rises on approval for its first-line cervical cancer treatment
data.description:
** Chinese antibody drugs developer Akeso rises ~6% to HK$86, highest since May 30

** Stock on course for third consecutive session of gains

** China's National Medical Products Administration approves supplemental New Drug Application for cadonilimab as first-line treatment for cervical cancer

** Says it is conducting more than 28 clinical trials of combination therapies that include cadonilimab, with indications covering gastric, liver, lung and other types of cancers

** YTD, stock up 36.8%

data.title: Philippines May Inflation Data Backs Case for Another Rate Cut — Market Talk
data.description: Philippines' May inflation print reinforces UOB's view that the BSP will cut the overnight reverse repurchase rate by another 25 bps later this month, UOB economist Loke Siew Ting writes in a note. The easing path will then depend on global tariff and financial market developments, as well as upcoming inflation and domestic growth data, she says. In May, headline inflation undershot the central bank's target range for the third straight month. UOB maintains its 2025 inflation forecast for the Philippines at 2.0%, given the non-monetary intervention by the national government. There are also expectations of lower global oil prices and a stronger peso for the rest of this year, the economist says.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Fresh Linear low density polyethylene (LLDPE Powder) offers in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa reported that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its LLDPE Powder (MG500026) grade at the USD 1285/mt levels.This offer is for June 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: China Carbon Neutral Development Appoints Ex-UN Adviser as Co-Chairman
data.description:
China Carbon Neutral Development appointed Chen Yonglan as co-chairman and non-executive director of the board, effective June 4, according to a Wednesday Hong Kong stock exchange filing.

Chen is a senior adviser to the United Nations and brings over 30 years of global experience in diplomacy, investment, and ESG. He previously held roles at the UNDP, Invest Sweden, French PE firm Eurazeo, and, most recently, Fullerton Fund Management, the filing said.

data.title: Copper Holds Advance on Supply Concerns
data.description:
Copper futures held near $4.90 per pound on Thursday, remaining up nearly 5% so far this week, as persistent supply-side disruptions and rising trade tensions fueled bullish sentiment.

Production setbacks at Teck Resources' operations in Chile are expected to last for several months, while mining activity at the Kamoa-Kakula project in the Democratic Republic of Congo has also been disrupted—tightening global supply.

Meanwhile, investor anxiety grew after President Trump signed an executive order doubling tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50%, sparking speculation that copper could be next.

The US is currently conducting an investigation into copper imports, adding to uncertainty.

However, upside momentum was tempered by weaker-than-expected manufacturing and services data from China, the world’s top copper consumer, raising concerns about demand softness.

data.title: Australia's PainChek drops most in nearly 8 years on FDA approval delay
data.description:
** PainChek plunges as much as ~31% to A$0.038; marks biggest intraday pct drop since June 18, 2017

** Stock hits lowest since May 7

** Medical devices maker flags potential delay in U.S. FDA clearance for its pain assessment app

** Expects clearance by mid-to-late September instead of the first half of 2025 anticipated earlier

** About 14.3 mln shares traded in stock's busiest day since February 18, 2021

** PCK last up 21.8%, taking YTD gains to 48.3%

data.title: Market Chatter: Banco Santander Names Chief Accounting Officer Replacement Amid Litigation Concerns
data.description:
Banco Santander reportedly replaced Alexsandro Broedel Lopes with Manuel Preto to take on the role of its new chief accounting officer, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing an internal memo it saw.

The move comes after Broedel, who was set to assume the Spanish lender's accounting chief role in June, was accused of breaching policies and funds diversion by Brazilian bank Itau Unibanco.

Preto's appointment is set to take effect on July 31, the report noted.

Banco Santander did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Palm Oil Retreats After Two-Day Rise
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil prices fell below MYR 3,920 per tonne, snapping a two-session gain amid signs of rising inventories.

According to Reuters, palm oil stocks likely rose for the third straight month in May, helped by a modest recovery in production.

Weakness in rival edible oils on the Chicago and Dalian exchanges also rattled sentiment.

Prices remain near their lowest in seven months, with the Malaysian Palm Oil Council projecting continued production growth through September, driven by replanting efforts and favorable weather.

Still, so far this week, the contracts were up around 1%, boosted by bets of strong demand from top buyer India after a recent cut in import duties.

India’s palm oil imports surged to a six-month high in May, as lower inventories and attractive pricing compared to soyoil and sunflower oil prompted refiners to boost purchases.

At the same time, Malaysia’s exports remained strong, with cargo surveyors estimating shipment growth of between 13.2% and 17.9% for May.

data.title: Australian Strategic Materials Refinances Korean Loan Facility, Sets New AU$10.2 Million Term
data.description:
Australian Strategic Materials refinanced its loan facility with the Korean Development Bank as part of a refinancing of existing Korean loan facilities associated with the company's Korean metals plant, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The refinanced loan will comprise a secured loan facility of up to AU$10.2 million, following a partial loan repayment of around AU$3.3 million, with a repayment date of June 10, 2026.

data.description:

** Order for mechanized cleaning contract of Guwahati railway station and its circulating area for a period of three years - DYNS

** Stock down 55.7%, YTD

($1 = 85.9170 Indian rupees)

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0252 GMT - Palm oil prices are lower in Asian trading, weighed by rising stock levels, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Malaysia's palm oil stocks in May likely rose 7.5% from the previous month to 2.01 million tons, a Bloomberg survey shows. However, robust export demand and resilience in external markets could offer a supportive backdrop, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support at 3,880 ringgit a ton and resistance at 3,977 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for August delivery is down 50 ringgit at 3,898 ringgit a ton.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0144 GMT - Copper edges higher in early Asian trade. The red metal has gained recently as supply disruptions mount, ANZ Research analysts write in a note. Canadian miner Teck Resources has been hit with production setbacks in Chile, and the issue could persist for several months, the analysts say. There is also mining disruption at the Kamoa-Kakula copper mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.15% higher at $9,635.50 a ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0130 GMT - The outperformance of Australian banking stocks over mining ones "is looking tired," Citi analysts say in a note. Since the start of 2024, Australia's banks have outperformed the S&P/ASX200 by roughly 27%, while materials stocks have underperformed the index by the same amount, the analysts say. The two sectors account for some 45% of the Australian market, so "getting the sector tilt right is one of the crucial calls for portfolio managers," they say. While the analysts aren't especially bullish on miners, they say investors should be mindful of their positioning. For banks, valuations look full and earnings expectations are being downgraded. Miners look better value and have some better earnings momentum, they say. Among miners, they highlight a buy rating on BHP. They say NAB and CBA are their top sell calls. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2345 GMT - Gold is trading steadily in the early morning Asian session, underpinned by weak U.S. economic data released overnight. Services activity unexpectedly contracted in May, while ADP data showed 37,000 jobs were created, the slowest pace of private-sector hiring in more than two years. The data has reinforced gold's appeal as a safe-haven asset, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia says in an email. The international backdrop is also favorable for gold, the research strategist adds, noting trade tensions between the U.S. and China. Spot gold is little changed at $3,374.86/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2243 GMT - It's only a matter of time before Greatland Gold acquires Antipa Minerals, reckons Euroz Hartleys. That's because Antipa's Minyari Gold project is located just 22 miles from Greatland Gold's Telfer processing plant in Australia. Analyst Michael Scantlebury notes Greatland's updated outlook for Telfer, which lays out how it expects to bridge the gap between now and when production starts from the Havieron deposit in FY 2028. Euroz Hartleys believes there would be ample processing capacity for Antipa's annual output of 3 million tons of ore from FY 2028. It also thinks "Antipa's Minyari Gold project ore tons are higher margin and lower risk" than what Greatland currently envisages. Euroz Hartleys lifts its price target on Antipa by 23% to A$0.98/share. Antipa ended Wednesday at A$0.66. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2034 GMT - Starbucks's newly appointed chief stores officer is already getting a promotion, with Mike Grams named as the coffee giant's COO. Grams, who previously worked for 30 years at Taco Bell, joined Starbucks in February as CEO Brian Niccol, also a Taco Bell alum, assembled his new leadership team. Starbucks last had a COO in 2022. Niccol is working to turn around the chain's sales, and Grams will have an expanded role in overseeing global store operations, development and supply chain. (heather.haddon@wsj.com ; @heatherhaddon)

2002 GMT - Lean hog and live cattle futures end higher after starting the day lower on the CME. Live cattle close up 1.3% to $2.125 a pound, and lean hogs rose 0.4% to $1.0575 a pound. Supporting both is a rate of slaughters trailing where they were at this time last year, says ADM Investor Services in a note. "The decreasing supply of beef, pork, and no increase in chicken is driving prices higher," says the firm. Cattle slaughter year-to-date is down from last year by over 830,000 head, and hog slaughter is off by 1.1 million head of hogs. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1906 GMT - Oil futures give back some of this week's gains in a volatile session. The 4.3 million barrel drawdown in U.S. crude inventories was offset by bigger-than-expected increases in gasoline and diesel stocks. Aramco cut its official July selling price to Asia by 20 cents a barrel, but raised prices for Europe and the U.S., while a report that Saudi Arabia favors further large monthly OPEC+ output increases after July added downward pressure on oil. President Trump said he had a good conversation with Russia's Vladimir Putin, but not one that will lead to immediate peace in the Russia-Ukraine conflict. He also said the U.S. needs a definitive answer soon from Iran on a nuclear deal. WTI settles down 0.9% at $62.85 a barrel, and Brent falls 1.2% to $64.86 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1904 GMT - U.S. natural gas prices are little changed ahead of tomorrow's EIA report, which is expected to show a sixth consecutive triple-digit increase in underground storage levels. "A historic spring injection season has flipped a 238 Bcf storage deficit versus five-year normals into a building storage surplus that may approach 200 Bcf by the 4th of July," Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics says in a note. The market may tighten rapidly into summer on higher power-sector and LNG demand, he adds. "During a midsummer heat wave, weekly injections may collapse to single digits." Nymex natural gas settles down 0.2%, or less than one cent at $3.716/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1848 GMT - Gold prices gain as comments from President Trump instill some new economic worries heading into the summer. Trump posted on his Truth Social account that China's President Xi was "extremely hard to make a deal with," also demanded Fed Chair Jerome Powell lower interest rates. "His comments suggest that a less-than-expected compromise may be on the horizon," says Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital Securities. Front-month gold futures settle up 0.7% to $3,373.50 a troy ounce, making it the second session out of the past three that gold has finished higher. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1748 GMT - Oil futures get little support from the bigger-than-expected 4.3 million barrel drawdown in U.S. crude oil stockpiles, which was accompanied by unexpectedly large increases in product stocks. "The big builds in gasoline and distillates were just head-scratchers, so much so that the market almost dismissed it out of hand," says Phil Flynn of the Price Futures Group. A drop in exports could have contributed to the 5.2 million barrel build in gasoline stocks, but the 1.2 million barrels-a-day decline in gasoline demand is questionable and will likely be adjusted. "Demand doesn't swing like that in gasoline," Flynn says. WTI crude is off 1.2% at $62.62 a barrel. Nymex gasoline is down 2.4% at $2.0291 a gallon. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1711 GMT - CBOT wheat climbs 2.1%. All three major row crops are higher after spending the morning paring overnight gains. Speculative buyers may be thinking about the bigger picture when looking at current grain futures prices, says agricultural consultant Sterling Smith. "Low prices do tend to attract some buyers," he says. "The dip well off highs likely inspired some short covering." Smith says that the market finishing the day on a strong note could "help the bull case," but otherwise welcome rainfall seen in Plains states is keeping urgency around adding risk premium low. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: ECB in Focus as Tariff, Geopolitical Concerns Linger
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

ECB rate decision, EU PPI; Germany manufacturing orders; trading update from Norwegian Air Shuttle

Opening Call:

European stock futures were treading water early Thursday on a mixed lead from Asian stock benchmarks; the dollar and Treasury yields edged higher; and oil futures and gold declined.

Equities:

European stock futures were barely changed early Thursday, with a broadly negative bias, ahead of the rate decision by the European Central Bank.

The ECB is widely expected to lower rates again for an eighth consecutive meeting, bringing the key rate down by two percentage points since this time last year.

U.S. stocks finished mixed Wednesday after weak economic data boosted investors' confidence that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates in the coming months.

The slightly softer economic data "increased the expectations that you'll see more rate cuts from the Fed this year," said Sinead Colton Grant, chief investment officer at BNY Wealth. "And that's supportive for stocks."

Meanwhile, geopolitical concerns persist. President Trump said Wednesday that Russian President Putin told him he would have to respond to Ukraine's recent attack, dampening prospects for immediate peace between Moscow and Kyiv. Trump also signed a sweeping travel ban on 12 countries, largely in the Middle East and Africa, and introduced more limited travel restrictions on seven others.

Forex:

Borrowing in dollars to invest in some emerging-market currencies looks more appealing now that the U.S. economy is unlikely to grow fast and the greenback is weakening, said TS Lombard's Daniel Von Ahlen. The carry trade is further supported by "the idea of a 'not too hot, not too cold' environment for risk appetite."

He favors the dollar as funding currency, but also likes the Chinese yuan and the Swiss franc, both low-interest-rate currencies. Among the attractive target currencies, Von Ahlen lists the Brazilian real, the Mexican peso, the Colombian peso, and the South African rand.

"Economic surprises are currently strongest in EM," he said.

Bonds:

U.S. Treasury yields edged higher, reversing Wednesday's decline. The decline in yields was likely the result of investors rejiggering bets on U.S. labor strength, Nuveen's Tony Rodriguez said.

The ADP report printed 37,000, versus the WSJ consensus of 110,000. The sharp yield decline that followed could reflect a realignment of expectations for Friday's payrolls, which are forecast to slow to 125,000 from 177,000.

Investors now have reasons to believe that "the risks to the consensus are more either balanced or to the downside," he said. Weak data challenge thoughts that 2Q activity could offset the 1Q contraction.

Energy:

Oil edged lower. There's a report "indicating that Saudi Arabia is exerting considerable pressure for a more aggressive increase in oil supply by OPEC+," XS.com's Antonio Di Giacomo said.

The "kingdom is actively seeking to regain market share, a goal that is driving its desire to accelerate production increases in the coming months." The country's strategy focuses on getting OPEC+ to boost production by at least 411,000 barrels per day in August and September, the analyst added.

Metals:

Gold declined, but may be underpinned by weak U.S. economic data released overnight. Services activity unexpectedly contracted in May, while ADP data showed the slowest pace of private-sector hiring in more than two years. The data has reinforced gold's appeal as a safe-haven asset, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said.

The international backdrop is also favorable for gold, the research strategist added, noting trade tensions between the U.S. and China.

--

Copper edged higher. The red metal has gained recently as supply disruptions mount, ANZ Research analysts said. Canadian miner Teck Resources has been hit with production setbacks in Chile, and the issue could persist for several months, they said.

There is also mining disruption at the Kamoa-Kakula copper mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump Loves Europe's Rate Cuts. Why the Fed Should Take Cover.

President Donald Trump reignited his campaign to get Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell to lower interest rates, holding up the European Central Bank as an example of how it should be done.

Well, the ECB is widely expected to lower rates again Thursday for an eighth consecutive meeting, bringing the key rate down by two percentage points since this time last year. The Fed, by contrast, delivered one point of cuts over the course of three meetings starting in September and has been on pause since December.

Cracks Are Appearing in the Tariff-Era Economy. Think Stagflation.

People watching the U.S. economy received a jolt of reality Wednesday through feedback from the men and women closest to its key growth engine, and from company bosses who manage the country's 170 million-strong workforce.

The message wasn't great. The latest data suggest slowing economic growth with the potential for faster inflation. It could be a toxic combination in that the inflation risk could make it harder for the Federal Reserve to lower rates to prop up the economy as it normally would.

Senate pushes Russia tariffs that could jack up energy prices for American consumers

Americans may soon feel the pinch at the pump and on their utility bills, even as the Trump administration and congressional Republicans remain laser focused on boosting U.S. energy production.

That's because a new bill gaining steam in the Senate could place severe tariffs on any country that imports oil CL00 BRN00, uranium and other energy products from Russia, with the potential for disrupting global energy markets and raising prices on everything from gasoline to electricity.

STMicroelectronics Shares Jump After CEO Says Chip Demand Is Recovering

Shares of Tesla and Apple supplier STMicroelectronics surged Wednesday after its chief executive said the company would hit guidance for the current quarter as demand for its semiconductors is picking up.

The European chip maker is forecasting revenue of about $2.71 billion in the second quarter, down more than 16% on year, but 7.7% higher compared with the first quarter. Its gross margin-a closely watched metric for companies in the semiconductor industry-should be about 33.4% in the second quarter, down from 40.1% a year earlier.

Trump Bans Citizens of 12 Countries From Traveling to U.S.

WASHINGTON-President Trump on Wednesday signed a sweeping travel ban on 12 countries, largely in the Middle East and Africa, and introduced more-limited travel restrictions on seven others, reintroducing a controversial immigration policy that came to define the early days of his first term.

The ban, which the White House announced, will completely bar travel to the U.S. by citizens of the following countries: Afghanistan, Myanmar (formerly Burma), Chad, The Republic of the Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Haiti, Iran, Libya, Somalia, Sudan and Yemen.

Kleenex-Owner Nears Roughly $3.5 Billion Sale of International Tissue Business

Kimberly-Clark is nearing a sale of its Kleenex and tissue businesses outside of North America for around $3.5 billion, part of an attempt by the maker of Scott toilet paper and Huggies diapers to focus on more profitable areas, according to people familiar with the matter.

Boeing Agrees to Pay $1.1 Billion to Avoid Prosecution for 737 MAX Crashes

Boeing will pay $1.1 billion to avoid prosecution for two crashes of its 737 MAX jets.

The agreement with the Justice Department requires the company to put $455 million toward strengthening its compliance, safety and quality programs, according to a Securities and Exchange Commission filing on Wednesday. Boeing will also give $444.5 million to the families of crash victims under the agreement, which was tentatively reached last month.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Thursday

00:01/IRL: May Ireland Services PMI

05:45/SWI: May Unemployment

06:00/SWE: 1Q Balance of Payments

06:00/GER: Apr Manufacturing turnover

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 12 AM ET: Mexico's Pemex to Trim Costs With Restructuring-Related Job Cuts | Senate ...
data.description:
Mexico's Pemex to Trim Costs With Restructuring-Related Job Cuts

Pemex is the world's most heavily indebted oil company, with debt of $101 billion at the end of the first quarter.

----

Senate pushes Russia tariffs that could jack up energy prices for American consumers

Americans may soon feel the pinch at the pump and on their utility bills, even as the Trump administration and congressional Republicans remain laser focused on boosting U.S. energy production.

----

Oil Futures Down Despite Big U.S. Stockpile Draw

Oil futures gave back some of this week's gains in a volatile session. WTI settled down 0.9%, and Brent fell 1.2%.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories fell by 4.3 million barrels, more than the estimated drop of 1.3 million barrels.

----

Saudi Arabia Trims Flagship Oil Selling Price to Asia

Saudi Arabia lowered the July price of its Arab Light crude for Asian buyers after OPEC+ decided to accelerate output hikes.

----

Meta Signs Nuclear Power Deal to Fuel Its AI Ambitions

The tech giant will buy the power generation of an Illinois nuclear plant under a deal with Constellation Energy.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Brookfield Renewable Partners, Repsol, Seatrium, and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

Viper Energy to Acquire Sitio Royalties in $4.1 Billion Stock Deal

Viper Energy will acquire Sitio Royalties in an all-stock deal valued at $4.1 billion, including debt.

----

Why Trump's Nuclear Plans Have So Far Failed to Boost Uranium Prices

Plans for a U.S. nuclear-power revival have excited uranium investors, stoking demand for shares in companies that produce the fuel.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil futures, Canada's uranium producers, Galp's Mopane discovery off Namibia and more in the latest Market Talks covering energy and utilities.

----

U.S. Proposes Lifting Oil and Gas Development Curbs at Alaska Reserve

The Interior Department is seeking to rescind 2024 restrictions.

----

Why oil's rally after Russia's 'Pearl Harbor' moment may be short-lived

Ukraine's audacious attack on Russian airfields sent a shockwave through the oil market, but analysts doubt the rally will last as OPEC+ accelerates production hikes.

data.title: Lagardère Issues EUR500 Million Bond due 2030
data.description:
French publishing company Lagardère issued a 500 million-euro bond due in June 2030, with an annual coupon of 4.75%, according to a Wednesday release.

The offering was more than three times oversubscribed. The proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of a bridge facility arranged during the June 2024 refinancing.

BNP Paribas, Crédit Agricole CIB, Natixis, and Société Générale CIB acted as global coordinators, alongside CIC, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and Santander CIB.

data.title: Powerhouse Ventures Sees Fiscal Year 2025 Maiden Cash Profit to Reach Over AU$200,000
data.description:
Powerhouse Ventures said it expects to achieve a maiden cash profit of over AU$200,000 in the fiscal year ending June 30, helped by new operating business units, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company expects group statutory profit for the fiscal year to be up by over 400% compared with a year ago.

data.title: Donaco Delays Scheme Meeting for Proposed Acquisition by On Nut Road; Shares Rose 5%
data.description:
Donaco International delayed the scheme meeting for shareholders to approve the proposed acquisition by investor and special purpose vehicle On Nut Road to Aug. 4 from its earlier date of June 3, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The delay is due to third parties not providing valuation inputs on time, postponing an independent expert's report, the filing said.

The first court hearing is set for June 20, and the second court date is set for Aug. 7, the filing added.

The scheme is set to be implemented on Aug. 19, the company said.

Shares of the company rose 5% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: South Korea to Fund Eco-Friendly, Autonomous Vehicle Tech via KRW34.1 Billion Investment
data.description:
The South Korean government will inject up to 34.1 billion won into around 30 research and development projects focused on eco-friendly and autonomous driving technologies, according to the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy on Wednesday.

The initiative includes the development of electric vehicles and hydrogen vehicle control systems, high-performance chips for software-defined vehicles, and AI models for self-driving cars. The move aims to strengthen the country's auto industry amid global economic headwinds and rising US trade tensions, according to the release.

data.title: AUD's Role as Economic Shock Absorber Might Fade — Market Talk
data.description: The AUD was floated in 1983 in the hope it would act as a shock absorber for the economy during times of global crisis. To be sure, currency devaluations have helped the economy navigate numerous crises over the past four decades. But Shane Oliver, chief economist at AMP, has warned that this trusted relationship could break down if the USD loses its safe haven status. The AUD may behave less like a shock absorber in a crisis by not falling as much as it normally would, he says. Time will tell, but if that's the case, more of the burden could fall on the RBA to help protect the economy during rough times, Oliver adds.(james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Sri Trang Gloves Completes Transfer of Business from Subsidiary
data.description:
Sri Trang Gloves Thailand completed the entire business transfer of its subsidiary, Sadao P.S. Rubber, to the company.

Sadao P.S. Rubber PS has registered the dissolution and will now proceed with the liquidation process, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: Apiam Animal CEO to Step Down
data.description:
Apiam Animal Health said that Chief Executive Chris Richards resigned, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company said it is searching for a new CEO.

Shares of the company fell about 1% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: The Generation Essentials Group and Black Spade Acquisition II Co Complete Business Combination
data.description:
HONG KONG, June 5, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - The Generation Essentials Groups Class A Ordinar'y Shares and Warrants to Begin Trading on the New York Stock Exchange and NYSE American on June 5, 2025, under Ticker Symbols "TGE" and "TGE WS", Respectively.

Jun 4, The Generation Essentials Group ("TGE"), an entity jointly established by AMTD Group, AMTD IDEA Group (NYSE: AMTD; SGX: HKB) and AMTD Digital Inc. (NYSE: HKD), and Black Spade Acquisition II Co (NASDAQ: BSII) ("Black Spade II") are pleased to confirm the completion of their previously announced business combination (the "Business Combination"). The listed company following the Business Combination is TGE, and its Class A ordinary shares and warrants will commence trading on the New York Stock Exchange and NYSE American under the ticker symbols "TGE" and "TGE WS", respectively, on June 5, 2025.

The announcement of the completion of the Business Combination comes after Black Spade II's shareholders voted to approve the transaction on May 30, 2025. As a result of the Business Combination, Black Spade II became a wholly owned subsidiary of TGE and is expected to be delisted from The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC.

Dr. Feridun Hamdullahpur, Chairman of the Board of The Generation Essentials Group, said: "This is about the successful listing of a comprehensive and diversified global media, entertainment, and hospitality play; the emergence of a worldwide operator in top-tier world "media" including L'Officiel and The Art Newspaper, "entertainment" including motion pictures, and "space" including coffee shops and hotels. I want to thank the Black Spade II team for their partnership and efficient handling of the deal process. With our two existing listed companies already under the NYSE banner, this third listing will offer a unique platform that brings together fashion, art, media, movies, entertainment, and hospitality under one global roof: TGE. We are now long-term partners with Black Spade, and there are numerous opportunities for cross-collaboration and mutual support on our journey towards building a world-leading media and entertainment company. On behalf of the Board of Directors, I would like to express my gratitude to everyone involved in bringing this innovative concept to fruition in record time."

Mr. Dennis Tam, Chairman and Co-CEO, Black Spade Acquisition II Co, said: "We are pleased to announce the successful completion of our merger, marking a significant milestone in bringing value for all our shareholders. In TGE's business footprint across fashion & art, media and hospitality, we saw an ideal match with our entertainment DNA. I would like to thank the TGE management team for their leadership, vision and efficiency in bringing this deal to fruition, and we look forward to the company's next chapter as a public company under their ticker symbol 'TGE', as they continue their exciting journey in the media and entertainment sector globally."

Additional information about the transaction, including a copy of the business combination agreement, is available in Black Spade II's Current Report on Form 8-K, filed on January 27, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") at www.sec.gov.

More information about the transaction is available in TGE's registration statement on Form F-4 which includes Black Spade II's proxy statement and TGE's prospectus in relation to the business combination, which was first filed with the SEC on April 11, 2025.

Photo caption:

(from left) Mr. Richard Taylor, Co-CEO, Black Spade Acquisition II Co, Mr. Kester Ng, Co-CEO, Black Spade Acquisition II Co, Mr. Dennis Tam, Chairman and Co-CEO, Black Spade Acquisition II Co, Mr. Lawrence Ho, Founder, Black Spade Capital, Mr. Calvin Choi, Founder and Director, The Generation Essentials Group, Dr. Feridun Hamdullahpur, Co-Chairperson of the Board of Directors, The Generation Essentials Group and Mr. Giampietro Baudo, CEO, The Generation Essentials Group

About The Generation Essentials Group (formerly known as World Media and Entertainment Universal Inc.)

The Generation Essentials Group, jointly established by AMTD Group, AMTD IDEA Group (NYSE: AMTD; SGX: HKB) and AMTD Digital Inc. (NYSE: HKD), is headquartered in France and focuses on global strategies and developments in multi-media, entertainment, and cultural affairs worldwide as well as hospitality and VIP services. TGE comprises L'Officiel, The Art Newspaper, movie and entertainment projects. Collectively, TGE is a diversified portfolio of media and entertainment businesses, and a global portfolio of premium properties.

About Black Spade Acquisition II Co

Black Spade Acquisition II Co ("Black Spade II") is a blank check company incorporated for the purpose of effecting a business combination (Special Purpose Acquisition Company or SPAC). Listed on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC , Black Spade II was founded by Black Spade Capital, which runs a global portfolio consisting of a wide spectrum of cross-border investments, and consistently seeks to add new investment projects and opportunities to its portfolio. Black Spade II is Black Spade Capital's second SPAC. Black Spade Capital's first SPAC completed its business combination with VinFast Auto Ltd., a Vietnamese electric vehicle company, in August 2023. At the time, it was the third largest ever de-SPAC by deal value (based on Dealogic data available through April 2024).

About AMTD Group

AMTD Group is a conglomerate with a core business portfolio spanning across media and entertainment, education and training, and premium assets and hospitality sectors.

About AMTD IDEA Group

AMTD IDEA Group (NYSE: AMTD; SGX: HKB) represents a diversified institution and digital solutions group connecting companies and investors with global markets. Its comprehensive one-stop business services plus digital solutions platform addresses different clients' diverse and inter-connected business needs and digital requirements across all phases of their life cycles. AMTD IDEA Group is uniquely positioned as an active super connector between clients, business partners, investee companies, and investors, connecting the East and the West. For more information, please visit www.amtdinc.com or follow us on X (formerly known as "Twitter") at @AMTDGroup.

About AMTD Digital Inc.

AMTD Digital Inc. (NYSE: HKD) is a comprehensive digital solutions platform headquartered in France. Its one-stop digital solutions platform operates key business lines including digital media, content and marketing services, investments as well as hospitality and VIP services. For AMTD Digital's announcements, please visit https://ir.amtdigital.net/investor-news.

data.title: Shree Marutinandan Tubes to Raise Up To INR490 Million Through Convertible Warrants
data.description:
Shree Marutinandan Tubes plans to raise up to 490 million Indian rupees through the issue of fully convertible warrants on a preferential basis, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company proposed to issue and allot up to 4,757,280 fully convertible warrants at an issue price of 103 rupees each, convertible into an equivalent number of fully paid-up equity shares of the company having a face value of 10 rupees in one or more tranches.

The tenure of the warrants will be up to 18 months from the date of allotment.
